properties.text:
Passthrough Mode  —  Topaz / SeedV2 / External Tool Skeleton

In passthrough mode, tiles are output at their NATIVE CROP SIZE with no scaling.
Your external tool (Topaz Gigapixel, SeedV2, etc.) upscales them by any factor.
TileStitch automatically detects the actual output size from the processed tiles.

HOW TO USE:
  1. Run this workflow to generate tile previews (tiles saved as PreviewImage output)
  2. Export/save individual tiles and process them with your external tool
  3. Load the processed tiles back and connect to TileCollect → TileStitch

For Topaz CLI:  topaz-gigapixel-ai --input tile_0.png --output tile_0_4x.png --scale 4
For SeedV2:     use the SeedV2 ComfyUI custom node to process each tile

TileStitch is resolution-agnostic: if Topaz upscaled 4x, the output canvas is 4x larger.
No need to specify any scale factor — it reads the actual tile dimensions automatically.

Grid: 4x4 (16 tiles) used here for maximum detail coverage.
Change to 2x2 or 3x3 for simpler workflows.

widgets_values:
Passthrough Mode  —  Topaz / SeedV2 / External Tool Skeleton

In passthrough mode, tiles are output at their NATIVE CROP SIZE with no scaling.
Your external tool (Topaz Gigapixel, SeedV2, etc.) upscales them by any factor.
TileStitch automatically detects the actual output size from the processed tiles.

HOW TO USE:
  1. Run this workflow to generate tile previews (tiles saved as PreviewImage output)
  2. Export/save individual tiles and process them with your external tool
  3. Load the processed tiles back and connect to TileCollect → TileStitch

For Topaz CLI:  topaz-gigapixel-ai --input tile_0.png --output tile_0_4x.png --scale 4
For SeedV2:     use the SeedV2 ComfyUI custom node to process each tile

TileStitch is resolution-agnostic: if Topaz upscaled 4x, the output canvas is 4x larger.
No need to specify any scale factor — it reads the actual tile dimensions automatically.

Grid: 4x4 (16 tiles) used here for maximum detail coverage.
Change to 2x2 or 3x3 for simpler workflows.

properties.text:
PHASE 2  —  After external processing:

Load your processed tiles back into ComfyUI using LoadImage nodes.
Connect them to TileCollect (tile_0 … tile_15 in row-major order).
Connect TileCollect.tiles → TileStitch.tiles
The stitched result will be at [scale_factor × original_size].
